GEN 1:1.text: WEB (World English Bible) is a modern public domain revision of the American Standard Version (1901).
'In the beginning, God created' - WEB updates ASV's Elizabethan English to modern English while preserving accuracy.
'God' here is Hebrew 'Elohim', a plural noun used with singular verbs, suggesting the Trinity. WEB capitalizes 'God' as a proper noun.

GEN 1:2.text: WEB 'now the earth was formless and empty' - Modern rendering of Hebrew 'tohu wabohu' (chaos and emptiness).
'God's Spirit' - WEB uses modern punctuation and phrasing while maintaining the Hebrew 'Ruach Elohim'.
'the deep' is Hebrew 'tehom', related to the Babylonian 'Tiamat'. WEB uses 'deep' rather than 'abyss' to avoid mythological connotations.

GEN 1:3.text: 'Let there be light' - Hebrew 'yehi or', literally 'be light'. WEB follows the Hebrew word order.
Light created before the sun (day 4), showing God is the source of all light.

GEN 1:26.text: 'Let us make' - The plural pronoun may indicate the Trinity, angels, or plural of majesty. WEB preserves the plural without interpretation.
'image' is Hebrew 'tselem', meaning representation or likeness. WEB distinguishes 'image' from 'likeness' in verse 27.
'man' is Hebrew 'adam', related to 'adamah' (ground). WEB uses 'man' as generic humanity, not just the individual Adam.

GEN 2:7.text: WEB 'Yahweh God formed man' - WEB restores the divine name 'Yahweh' where ASV used 'Jehovah'.
'breathed into his nostrils the breath of life' - WEB preserves the intimate image of God breathing life into humanity.
'living soul' - Hebrew 'nephesh chayyah'. WEB uses 'soul' here, though 'nephesh' also means creature or being.

GEN 3:15.text: WEB 'I will put hostility between you and the woman' - Modern phrasing of the ancient curse and promise.
'he will bruise your head' - The seed of the woman (Messiah) will ultimately defeat the serpent (Satan).
This verse is called the 'Protoevangelium' - the first gospel promise. WEB translators recognized its messianic significance.

GEN 12:1.text: 'Get out of your country' - Hebrew 'lech lecha', literally 'go for yourself' or 'go to yourself'. WEB captures the command and purpose.
Abram was 75 years old when he left Haran (v. 4). WEB narrative shows God's call comes regardless of age.

GEN 15:6.text: 'believed' - Hebrew 'he'emin', from root 'aman' (firm, faithful). WEB 'believed' captures the sense of trust and reliance.
'counted it to him for righteousness' - Hebrew 'chashab', to reckon or impute. WEB uses 'counted' for legal/accounting terminology.
Quoted by Paul in Romans 4:3, Galatians 3:6, and James 2:23 as foundational for justification by faith.

EXO 3:14.text: WEB 'I AM WHO I AM' - Modern rendering of Hebrew 'Ehyeh asher ehyeh', the divine self-revelation.
'Yahweh' - WEB uses the scholarly reconstruction of the divine name rather than 'Jehovah' or 'LORD'.
The name indicates God's self-existence and eternal nature. WEB captures this with the timeless 'I AM'.

EXO 20:3.text: WEB 'You shall have no other gods before me' - First Commandment in modern English.
'before me' means 'in my presence' or 'besides me' - exclusive worship of Yahweh alone.

EXO 20:7.text: Third Commandment: 'You shall not take the name of Yahweh your God in vain.' WEB uses 'Yahweh' instead of 'the LORD'.
'in vain' is Hebrew 'shav', emptiness, falsehood, or worthlessness. WEB captures the prohibition against empty use.

PSA 23:1.text: WEB 'Yahweh is my shepherd' - WEB restores the divine name instead of 'LORD' in small caps.
'I shall lack nothing' - Modern rendering of Hebrew 'lo echsar' - complete provision.
David wrote this psalm based on his experience as a shepherd boy (1 Samuel 16:11).

PSA 23:4.text: WEB 'Even though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death' - Modern phrasing preserving Hebrew imagery.
'your rod and your staff, they comfort me' - The shepherd's tools for protection and guidance.
'comfort' is Hebrew 'nacham', to console or have compassion. WEB emphasizes divine comfort in danger.

PSA 51:10.text: 'Create' is Hebrew 'bara', used only for divine creation. WEB preserves this strong term for spiritual renewal.
'right spirit' - Hebrew 'ruach nachon', a steadfast or established spirit. WEB 'right' means 'correct' or 'proper'.
David wrote this psalm after his sin with Bathsheba (2 Samuel 12). WEB preserves the personal confession.

PSA 119:11.text: 'I have hidden your word in my heart' - Hebrew 'tsaphan', to treasure or store up. WEB captures the sense of valuing and protecting God's Word.
'heart' in Hebrew includes mind, will, and emotions. WEB 'heart' encompasses the whole inner being.
This acrostic psalm has 22 sections for each letter of the Hebrew alphabet. WEB preserves the structure.

ISA 7:14.text: WEB 'the virgin shall conceive' - Modern translation of Hebrew 'almah' with Greek 'parthenos' understanding.
'Immanuel' means 'God is with us' - the name given to the promised child.
Quoted in Matthew 1:23 as fulfilled in Jesus' birth. WEB Matthew explicitly connects this to Christ.

ISA 9:6.text: WEB 'For a child is born to us... and the government will be on his shoulders' - Messianic prophecy.
'Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace' - Four divine titles of the Messiah.
'Mighty God' is Hebrew 'El Gibbor'. WEB capitalizes 'God', identifying the child as divine.

ISA 53:5.text: WEB 'he was pierced for our transgressions' - Some Hebrew manuscripts support 'pierced' (Mechon Mamre).
'the punishment that brought us peace was on him' - Substitutionary atonement: he suffered for our sin.
This chapter is the great Messianic prophecy of the suffering Servant. WEB preserves its application to Christ.

MAT 1:23.text: WEB 'Behold, the virgin shall be with child, and shall give birth to a son' - Fulfilling Isaiah 7:14.
'They shall call his name Immanuel, which is, being interpreted, God with us' - Matthew explains the Hebrew name.

MAT 5:3.text: WEB 'Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the Kingdom of Heaven' - First Beatitude.
'poor in spirit' - Greek 'ptochos to pneumati' - spiritually destitute, recognizing their need for God.

MAT 5:44.text: WEB 'Love your enemies' - Greek 'agapao', self-sacrificing love. WEB distinguishes this from 'phileo' (friendship love).
'pray for those who persecute you' - Revolutionary command, unique to Christianity.

MAT 6:9.text: WEB 'Our Father in heaven, may your name be kept holy' - The Lord's Prayer in modern English.
'your Kingdom come, your will be done on earth as it is in heaven' - Prayer for God's rule on earth.

MAT 6:33.text: WEB 'But seek first God's Kingdom, and his righteousness' - Priority of God's reign.
'righteousness' is Greek 'dikaiosyne', right standing with God. WEB distinguishes this from works-righteousness.

MAT 11:28.text: WEB 'Come to me, all you who labor and are heavily burdened' - Jesus' invitation to the weary.
'I will give you rest' - Greek 'anapauso' - rest, refreshment, cessation from labor.

MAT 28:19.text: WEB 'Go, and make disciples of all nations' - The Great Commission.
'baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit' - Trinitarian formula.

JHN 1:1.text: WEB 'In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God' - The eternal Word.
'the Word was God' - The Word shares the divine nature. Greek word order emphasizes 'God' as predicate.

JHN 1:14.text: WEB 'The Word became flesh, and lived among us' - The incarnation: God became human.
'lived' is Greek 'eskenosen', tabernacled or pitched tent. WEB captures the imagery of God's presence.

JHN 3:3.text: WEB 'Most certainly, I tell you, unless one is born anew, he can't see God's Kingdom' - Born from above.
Spiritual rebirth required to see God's kingdom.

JHN 3:16.text: WEB 'For God so loved the world, that he gave his one and only Son' - The extent of God's love.
'whoever believes in him should not perish, but have eternal life' - Universal offer of salvation through faith.

JHN 14:6.text: WEB 'I am the way, the truth, and the life' - Jesus' exclusive claim as the only path to God.
'No one comes to the Father, except through me' - Salvation is found only in Christ.

ROM 3:23.text: WEB 'for all have sinned, and fall short of the glory of God' - Universal human sinfulness.
'fall short' - Greek 'hysterountai' - lacking, coming behind the standard of God's glory.

ROM 5:8.text: WEB 'But God demonstrates his own love toward us, in that while we were yet sinners, Christ died for us'.
God's love is demonstrated through action: Christ died for us while we were still his enemies.

ROM 6:23.text: WEB 'For the wages of sin is death, but the free gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord'.
Contrast: earned wages (death) vs. free gift (eternal life). We earn death but receive life by grace.

ROM 8:28.text: WEB 'We know that all things work together for good for those who love God' - God's providence.
The promise is for believers who love God and are called according to his purpose.

EPH 2:8.text: WEB 'for by grace you have been saved through faith, and that not of yourselves; it is the gift of God'.
Salvation is entirely God's work: grace is the source, faith is the channel, the gift is free.

PHP 4:13.text: WEB 'I can do all things through Christ, who strengthens me' - Strength for every circumstance.
Context (vv. 11-12) shows Paul means contentment in all situations, not ability to do anything.

HEB 4:12.text: WEB 'For the word of God is living and active, and sharper than any two-edged sword'.
God's Word penetrates deeply, discerning thoughts and intentions of the heart.

1JN 1:9.text: WEB 'If we confess our sins, he is faithful and righteous to forgive us the sins'.
'cleanse us from all unrighteousness' - Complete forgiveness and cleansing through confession.
